evals.prompt: Every Friday I send a competitor social pulse. Compare our LinkedIn, Instagram, and Facebook activity against these 5 competitors for the last 7 days.
evals.expected_output: Should check for product-marketing.md first. Should use the same seven-day window for every account and compare like-for-like platforms rather than blending platform metrics into one leaderboard. Should compare cadence, format mix, engagement efficiency, follower or audience context when available, top post patterns, gaps, and practical tests for the next week.
assertions: Uses the same 7-day window for all accounts
Identifies top post patterns and gaps
Recommends tests for the next week

evals.prompt: Our CEO wants one slide: are we ahead or behind competitors on LinkedIn?
evals.expected_output: Should produce an executive read without a simplistic winner ranking. Should normalize by followers, posts, reach, or impressions when those denominators are available, state the denominator used for each rate, and label confidence if public or partial data limits the comparison. Should summarize where the user is ahead, behind, or inconclusive and identify the most important action to take next.
assertions: Summarizes ahead, behind, and inconclusive areas
Identifies the most important next action

evals.prompt: A competitor's launch video blew up on YouTube yesterday. Should we copy their approach?
evals.expected_output: Should separate a one-off spike from a repeatable content pattern. Should analyze the video's title or hook, thumbnail, format, proof points, CTA, timing, audience fit, and relationship to the competitor's launch narrative. Should recommend adapting the underlying pattern to the user's positioning and audience instead of copying the content directly.
assertions: Separates one-off spike from repeatable pattern
Connects the post to the launch narrative

evals.prompt: We're heavy on Instagram. Three competitors are on Instagram too, but one is LinkedIn-only. Compare everyone.
evals.expected_output: Should recommend like-for-like platform comparisons and refuse to produce a single cross-platform engagement-rate leaderboard that mixes Instagram and LinkedIn. Should produce per-platform scorecards, explain why metric definitions and audience behavior are not directly comparable, and suggest adding platform-matched competitors if the user wants a LinkedIn benchmark.

evals.prompt: I grabbed screenshots of 6 competitor posts from the last month. Who is winning?
evals.expected_output: Should call out that 6 screenshot posts from a month is a small and likely uneven sample. Should label confidence as low, avoid declaring a winner, and separate visible observations from rankings. Should recommend collecting a larger, consistent sample across the same date range, platform, and metrics before drawing conclusions.
assertions: Specifies matching date range, platform, and metrics

evals.prompt: Add this new TikTok competitor to our weekly benchmark tracker and include them in the next report.
evals.expected_output: Should treat modifying a tracker or external system as a write action requiring confirmation before proceeding. Should confirm the competitor profile, platform, benchmark set, and reporting window. Should explain how the new competitor will be included in future comparisons and ensure they are compared only against platform-matched accounts.
assertions: Identifies tracker modification as a write action
Confirms benchmark set and reporting window
Explains how the competitor enters future reports
